reforms.description: The government will establish 17 'Creative Economy Innovation Centres' in major cities by 2015 to support start-ups through education, technology development, and financing.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These centres aim to enhance the entrepreneurial ecosystem, providing necessary resources for start-ups to thrive, which can lead to increased innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government will establish 17 'Creative Economy Innovation Centres' by 2015.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change aimed at fostering innovation and entrepreneurship across the country, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The government plans to establish 17 'Creative Economy Innovation Centres' to support start-ups through education, technology development, and financing.

reforms.description: The government aims to improve the innovation framework by expanding the role of universities and upgrading government research institutes to enhance the return on investment in innovation.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing the innovation framework, the government seeks to increase productivity and efficiency in research and development, which can lead to higher economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to improve the innovation framework by expanding the role of universities.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it targets the enhancement of the innovation ecosystem, which is essential for long-term economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Improve the innovation framework by expanding the role of universities, upgrading government research institutes.

reforms.description: The government is encouraging universities to expand their role in innovation by strengthening links between research institutes in government, business, and academia, and promoting the labor mobility of researchers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Strengthening collaboration between universities and industry enhances knowledge transfer, which can lead to increased innovation and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is actively promoting the labor mobility of researchers and collaboration between sectors.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant effort to enhance the role of universities in innovation, which is crucial for fostering a more dynamic and competitive economy.
reforms.source_quote: The government is encouraging universities to appoint professionals from the business sector as faculty members and dispatch university faculty to industrial research centres.

reforms.description: The government unveiled a comprehensive three-year innovation plan aimed at fostering a creative economy, which includes significant investments in start-ups and venture businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to enhance the start-up ecosystem, which is crucial for job creation and technological advancement, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to the plan and is actively investing in start-ups and venture businesses.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural shift towards a more innovation-driven economy, with significant implications for job creation and economic dynamism.
reforms.source_quote: The government unveiled a comprehensive three-year innovation plan aimed at fostering a creative economy, which includes significant investments in start-ups and venture businesses.

reforms.description: The government launched a three-year innovation plan aimed at fostering a creative economy by enhancing the role of universities in business R&D and increasing international collaboration in R&D.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving the innovation system and enhancing collaboration between universities and businesses, the plan aims to boost productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The plan was launched to address structural weaknesses and promote innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This plan represents a significant structural change in Korea's approach to innovation, aiming to integrate universities more closely with industry and enhance R&D collaboration.
reforms.source_quote: The government unveiled a three-year innovation plan at the end of February 2014.

reforms.description: Korea's government aims to enhance the quality of universities and strengthen their links with firms to improve the efficiency of the innovation system, which is crucial for translating high R&D spending into productivity growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving university quality and collaboration with industry is expected to enhance innovation outputs and productivity.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has outlined plans to upgrade universities and strengthen links with firms.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform addresses systemic issues in the education sector that directly impact innovation capacity and economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: Upgrading the quality of universities and strengthening their links with firms is essential for improving the efficiency of Korea’s innovation system.

reforms.description: The government is enhancing public support for business-sector R&D through a balanced approach that includes both tax incentives and direct support, aiming to address market failures in R&D investment.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Increasing public support for R&D is expected to stimulate private sector investment and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has committed to enhancing public support for business-sector R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in the approach to R&D funding, aiming to maximize the impact of public investment on private sector innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Public support for business-sector R&D is justified by market failures that keep R&D spending below the socially-optimal level.

reforms.description: The Korean government launched a comprehensive plan in May 2013 to enhance the role of venture businesses, which includes promoting angel investment, introducing crowd-funding, and developing the M&A market for venture businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: The plan aims to increase investment in start-ups and venture businesses, which are crucial for innovation and job creation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched the plan in May 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a structural change in the support for venture businesses, aiming to shift the economy towards a more innovation-driven model.
reforms.source_quote: To address these weaknesses, the government has launched initiatives to: i) promote angel investment and introduce crowd-funding.

reforms.description: The government is improving the infrastructure for venture businesses and start-ups by launching a start-up platform and encouraging cooperation between government research institutes and venture businesses.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing infrastructure for start-ups facilitates innovation and entrepreneurship, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government launched a start-up platform in 2013.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it enhances the support system for start-ups, but it is part of a broader initiative rather than a standalone major reform.
reforms.source_quote: The government is also taking measures to expand and improve the infrastructure for venture businesses and start-ups.

reforms.description: The government has made an important start in improving vocational education by introducing Meister schools, which emphasize workplace training based on the German model of training master craftsmen.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing vocational education is expected to improve the skills of the workforce, thereby increasing productivity and innovation capacity in the economy.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of Meister schools represents a significant shift towards vocational training, which is crucial for addressing labor market mismatches and enhancing the skill set of the workforce.
reforms.source_quote: The government has made an important start in this direction in 2008 by introducing Meister schools, which emphasize workplace training, based on the German model of training master craftsmen.

reforms.description: The government aims to improve universities and expand their cooperation with the business sector in R&D, while increasing the contribution of government research institutes.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing cooperation between universities and businesses is expected to facilitate knowledge transfer and innovation, leading to increased productivity and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government aims to improve universities and expand their cooperation with the business sector in R&D.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to strengthen the link between academia and industry, which is crucial for fostering innovation and economic development.
reforms.source_quote: Improve universities and expand their co-operation with the business sector in R&D, while increasing the contribution of government research institutes.
